children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations under subsection (d), the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency shall, in consultation with the Secretary of Agriculture and the Biomass Research and Development Technical Advisory Committee established under section 8605 of title 7, establish a program, to be known as the “Advanced Biofuel Technologies Program”, to demonstrate advanced technologies for the production of alternative transportation fuels.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Priority
children.label: b Priority
children.text: In carrying out the program under subsection (a), the Administrator shall give priority to projects that enhance the geographical diversity of alternative fuels production and utilize feedstocks that represent 10 percent or less of ethanol or biodiesel fuel production in the United States during the previous fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: to develop not less than 4 different conversion technologies for producing cellulosic biomass ethanol; and

children.label: B
children.text: to develop not less than 5 technologies for coproducing value-added bioproducts (such as fertilizers, herbicides, and pesticides) resulting from the production of biodiesel fuel.

children.label: A
children.text: conducted based on a merit-reviewed, competitive process; and

children.label: B
children.text: subject to the cost-sharing requirements of .section 16352 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $110,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2005 through 2009.
